tools.name: query
tools.description: Execute a SQL statement against the project's ScalixNova PostgreSQL database. Accepts any single statement (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DDL) with optional positional parameters ($1, $2, ...). Returns JSON with column names, rows, row count, and a cost breakdown. Writes are permanent — use create

tools.name: get_schema
tools.description: Get the full database schema including tables, columns, types, indexes, foreign keys, and PII classifications.

tools.name: get_table
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific table including columns, indexes, foreign keys, and sample values.

tools.name: get_relationships
tools.description: Get the relationship map showing how tables are connected via foreign keys.

tools.name: search_columns
tools.description: Search for columns by name or type across all tables.

tools.name: list_pii
tools.description: List all columns classified as containing personally identifiable information (PII).

tools.name: create_sandbox
tools.description: Create an isolated ScalixNova database branch (instant copy-on-write sandbox) for safe experimentation — run queries and migrations against it without touching production data. Returns a scoped token for the sandbox; destroy it with destroy_sandbox or let the TTL expire.

tools.name: destroy_sandbox
tools.description: Destroy a sandbox environment and revoke its token.

tools.name: optimize_query
tools.description: Analyze a SQL query and return optimization suggestions including index recommendations and query rewrites.

tools.name: nl_to_sql
tools.description: Translate a plain-language question into a candidate SQL query using pattern-matching against the live schema (no AI model — simple questions only: counts, averages, filtered selects on a named table). Returns the SQL without executing it, with a confidence score; low confidence means the table was

tools.name: scalix_db_query
tools.description: Execute a SQL query against the project database. Returns columns, rows, row count, and cost breakdown. Destructive statements (DROP/TRUNCATE/bulk DELETE) require a two-step confirmation: the first call returns code CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED with a confirmation_token — re-call with that value in confirm

tools.name: scalix_db_schema
tools.description: Get the full database schema including all tables, columns, types, and relationships.

tools.name: scalix_db_table
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific database table including columns, indexes, and foreign keys.

tools.name: scalix_db_sandbox
tools.description: Create an isolated database sandbox (instant branch) for safe experimentation. Returns the sandbox id and branch details.

tools.name: scalix_db_migrate
tools.description: Apply a database migration by version number.

tools.name: scalix_storage_list
tools.description: List storage buckets, or list objects in a specific bucket with optional prefix filter.

tools.name: scalix_storage_upload
tools.description: Upload content to object storage. Provide the content as a base64-encoded string.

tools.name: scalix_storage_download
tools.description: Download an object from storage. Returns the content as base64.

tools.name: scalix_storage_create_bucket
tools.description: Create a new Scalix Storage bucket in the project (S3-compatible object storage). Bucket names must be unique within the project. Setting public=true makes every object in the bucket readable without authentication — leave it false (the default) and use presigned URLs for private sharing.

tools.name: scalix_fn_list
tools.description: List all deployed serverless functions.

tools.name: scalix_fn_deploy
tools.description: Deploy a Scalix Function — serverless, per-request billed, running in isolated microVMs — from a container image. Invoke it with scalix_fn_invoke once deployed.

tools.name: scalix_fn_invoke
tools.description: Invoke a deployed function with a JSON payload.

tools.name: scalix_kv_get
tools.description: Read a single value from the project's key-value store by exact key. Returns the stored value, or a not-found error if the key does not exist or its TTL has expired. Use scalix_kv_list to discover keys by prefix first.

tools.name: scalix_kv_set
tools.description: Set a key-value pair with optional TTL.

tools.name: scalix_kv_list
tools.description: List keys in the key-value store, optionally filtered by prefix.

tools.name: scalix_ai_infer
tools.description: Run AI inference on Scalix AI — the Scalix Lumio model family. Sends a prompt and returns the model's response in the OpenAI-compatible chat-completions format; tokens are billed to the project's credit pool. Discover available model IDs with scalix_ai_models.

tools.name: scalix_ai_models
tools.description: List the AI models available to the project — the Scalix Lumio family and any models enabled for the org — with their IDs for use in scalix_ai_infer.

tools.name: scalix_events_publish
tools.description: Publish an event to a topic. Provide an event_type (a short name for the kind of event, e.g. 'user.created') and the data payload.

tools.name: scalix_events_topics
tools.description: List the event-bus topics in the project, with each topic's ID and name. Use this to find or verify a topic_id before publishing with scalix_events_publish; publishing to a non-existent topic fails.

tools.name: scalix_cron_create
tools.description: Create a cron schedule to run a function or webhook on a timer.

tools.name: scalix_compute_deploy
tools.description: COMING SOON — not available yet. Long-running container deployments are not built yet; this returns 501. To run containers today use scalix_run_deploy (Scalix Run services) or scalix_fn_deploy (Functions).

tools.name: scalix_compute_list
tools.description: COMING SOON — not available yet. Container deployments are not built yet; this returns 501. Use scalix_run_list / scalix_fn_list instead.

tools.name: scalix_compute_scale
tools.description: COMING SOON — not available yet. Container deployments are not built yet; this returns 501. Scale a Scalix Run service with scalix_run_scale instead.

tools.name: scalix_compute_delete
tools.description: COMING SOON — not available yet. Container deployments are not built yet; this returns 501. Delete a Scalix Run service with scalix_run_delete instead.

tools.name: scalix_sandbox_run
tools.description: Execute code in an isolated sandbox. Supports Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Bash. Returns stdout/stderr.

tools.name: scalix_domain_add
tools.description: Add a custom domain to the project. Returns DNS records to configure.

tools.name: scalix_domain_list
tools.description: List all custom domains and their SSL/verification status.

tools.name: scalix_domain_verify
tools.description: Verify DNS configuration for a custom domain and provision SSL certificate.

tools.name: scalix_build_create
tools.description: Create a new container image build from a Git repository or Dockerfile.

tools.name: scalix_build_status
tools.description: Get the status and logs of a build.

tools.name: scalix_project_create
tools.description: Create a new project within an organization. Returns the project ID and API credentials.

tools.name: scalix_project_list
tools.description: List every project in an organization, with each project's ID, name, and slug. Use this to resolve a project ID before calling project-scoped tools. Requires admin scope on the organization.

tools.name: scalix_auth_configure
tools.description: Configure end-user authentication for the project — require MFA, require email confirmation, and set the allowed auth providers.

tools.name: scalix_status
tools.description: Get the current health status of the Scalix Cloud platform including all service components.

tools.name: scalix_usage
tools.description: Get current usage metrics and costs for a project.

tools.name: scalix_run_deploy
tools.description: Deploy a long-running container service on Scalix Run from a container image. Creates a new service, or a new revision of an existing service with the same name, with health checks, a public HTTPS URL, and autoscaling between min_instances and max_instances (min 0 = scale-to-zero when idle). Billed

tools.name: scalix_run_list
tools.description: List all deployed container services in the project.

tools.name: scalix_run_scale
tools.description: Change the autoscaling bounds of an existing Scalix Run service without redeploying it. Raising min_instances keeps instances warm (no cold starts, higher cost); min_instances 0 enables scale-to-zero when idle. Takes effect immediately. Find service IDs with scalix_run_list.

tools.name: scalix_run_rollback
tools.description: Roll a Scalix Run service back to an earlier revision — omit 'revision' to return to the immediately previous one. Traffic switches to the target revision; the replaced revision is kept and can be redeployed later. Use scalix_run_list to see services and their current revisions.

tools.name: scalix_search
tools.description: Search the Scalix original-source index — a curated set of original publishers (newsrooms, wires, regulators), not the open web. Returns originals only by default (syndicated copies collapsed to the source that published first), with provenance: source, published date, region, and canonical flag.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "query" is 624 chars, far longer than this server's median (108). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Execute a SQL statement against the project's ScalixNova PostgreSQL database. Accepts any single statement (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DDL) with optional p…

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "query" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "scalix_project_create" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a new project within an organization. Returns the project ID and API credentials.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "scalix_project_list" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List every project in an organization, with each project's ID, name, and slug. Use this to resolve a project ID before calling project-scoped tools. Requires ad…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: nl_to_sql · Private data: query, get_schema, create_sandbox, destroy_sandbox, scalix_db_query, scalix_db_schema, scalix_db_table, scalix_db_sandbox, scalix_db_migrate, scalix_storage_create_bucket, scalix_ai_infer, scalix_domain_add, scalix_project_create · Public sink: scalix_storage_upload, scalix_events_publish, scalix_cron_create, scalix_auth_configure · Destructive: query, create_sandbox, destroy_sandbox, nl_to_sql, scalix_db_query, scalix_ai_infer, scalix_cron_create, scalix_compute_deploy, scalix_compute_scale, scalix_compute_delete, scalix_sandbox_run, scalix_run_deploy, scalix_run_list, scalix_run_scale, scalix_run_rollback.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: nl_to_sql · Private data: query, get_schema, create_sandbox, destroy_sandbox, scalix_db_query, scalix_db_schema, scalix_db_table, scalix_db_sandbox, scalix_db_migrate, scalix_storage_create_bucket, scalix_ai_infer, scalix_domain_add, scalix_project_create · Public sink: scalix_storage_upload, scalix_events_publish, scalix_cron_create, scalix_auth_configure · Destructive: query, create_sandbox, destroy_sandbox, nl_to_sql, scalix_db_query, scalix_ai_infer, scalix_cron_create, scalix_compute_deploy, scalix_compute_scale, scalix_compute_delete, scalix_sandbox_run, scalix_run_deploy, scalix_run_list, scalix_run_scale, scalix_run_rollback

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

remediation.title: Tool description changed since last scan
remediation.detail: Tool "scalix_storage_create_bucket" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Create a new storage bucket." → after: "Create a new Scalix Storage bucket in the project (S3-compatible object storage). Bucket names must be unique within the project. Setting public=true makes every object in the bucket readable without authentication — leave it false (the default) and use presigned URLs for private sharing."

evidence.snippet: before: "Delete a container deployment." → after: "Permanently delete a compute deployment: stops all running instances, releases their resources, and ends billing for it. Irreversible — the deployment and its configuration are not recoverable; recreate with scalix_compute_deploy if needed. Verify the deployment_id with scalix_compute_list first."
